predictions.description: decrypt(encrypt(x, pw), pw) === x, including multi-byte UTF-8 (é ö ✓ —). Ciphertext is random per run, so the ONLY stable contract is the round trip plus format.
Ciphertext is exactly three space-separated base64 blobs (salt, IV, data) — pinned via in-bot regex-is-match because the blobs themselves are random.
Encrypting the same plaintext twice yields DIFFERENT ciphertexts (fresh random salt + IV each call) — equal outputs would mean the salt/IV generation broke.
Both buried failures (wrong password → 'incorrect password or corrupted data'; malformed 2-part input → format error) fail BEFORE writing the output variable — the sentinel survives.
